sections.paragraph:
m.-

(1) a seer, a prophet, a word of the silver age of the Hebrew language [also of ancient use; see 1Sa 9:9 ], of the same meaning as נָבִיא 1Ch 21:9 25:5 29:29 .

(2) [“Segolate (like רֹאֶח Isa 28:7), and abstr.”] i.q. חָזוּת No. 3 (which see), a covenant, Isa 28:15 on which passage see my Commentary: [“a vision, hence a covenant”].
